paper_title: Synthesis and Characterization of MWCNT/Diatomite Composite by C-CVD

methods.method_name: Synthesis of MWCNT/Diatomite Composite by C-CVD
methods.description: This method describes the synthesis of multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) grown directly on the surface of natural diatomite using the catalytic chemical vapor deposition (C-CVD) technique. The process involves preparing metal catalyst precursors (cobalt and nickel nitrates), impregnating the diatomite support, drying, and then reacting a propane-butane gas mixture at high temperatures under an inert atmosphere.

steps.description: We prepared two solutions with three different concentrations (0.5, 1.0 and 1.5 M) of Co(NO 3 ) 2 ·6H 2 O; Ni(NO 3 ) 2 ·6H 2 O, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare metal nitrate solutions for catalyst precursor.

steps.description: Then, 0.5 g of diatomaceous earth was impregnated with an alcohol solution of Co(NO 3 ) 2 ·6H 2 O; Ni(NO 3 ) 2 ·6H 2 O, respectively...
steps.searchable_summary: Impregnate diatomite carrier with metal catalyst precursor solutions.

steps.description: ...and dried for 13-15 min at a temperature of 80-100 • C, necessary and sufficient for the evaporation of water and alcohol.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry the impregnated diatomite catalyst precursor.
outputs: Dried metal-containing catalyst powder on diatomite

steps.description: As a result, a metal-containing catalyst (diatomite) was obtained in the form of a powder. Then, it was placed in a reactor and heated in an argon flow to 400-500 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Load catalyst into reactor and heat under inert atmosphere.
outputs: Catalyst in reactor at 400-500 °C under argon flow

steps.description: Then, the temperature for the synthesis of carbon nanotubes was increased (650 • C, 700 • C, 750 • C and 800 • C), and a propane-butane gas mixture was supplied for 30 min at a flow rate of 90 cm 3 /min.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct catalytic chemical vapor deposition (CVD) reaction for CNT synthesis.
outputs: Synthesized MWCNT/diatomite composite within the reactor

steps.description: Then, the reactor was cooled to room temperature in argon for 1-1.5 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Cool the reactor and synthesized composite under inert atmosphere.
outputs: Synthesized MWCNT/diatomite composite at room temperature

methods.description: This method details the analytical techniques used to characterize the synthesized MWCNT/diatomite composite and the natural diatomite support. Characterization includes examining morphology using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM), analyzing structural properties using Raman Spectroscopy, and determining the crystalline phases of the natural diatomite using X-ray Diffraction (XRD).

steps.description: The obtained samples were studied by scanning electron microscopy on Quanta 3D 200i (FEI company, Hillsboro, OR, USA)...
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire SEM images and morphological data of samples.

steps.description: ...and Raman spectroscopy on Solver Spectrum (NT-MDT, Moscow, Russia) using a 473 nm laser.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire Raman spectra of samples to analyze structure.
outputs: Structural information (e.g., D/G band ratios, defects)

steps.description: Figure 3 represents the X-ray diffraction pattern of natural diatomite, which shows that diatomite contains quartz...
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire XRD pattern of natural diatomite for phase identification.
